name: Fuelling Foodies: The Food Marketing Podcast
description:
Fuelling Foodies is for the real food & drink founders who want to scale without the fluff. Hosted by Kate Williams, founder of KW Marketing, this podcast gives you the raw truth about growing your food business. Forget generic advice – this is about real stories, struggles, and breakthroughs.

Every episode will dive deep into what it actually takes to build and grow a food business. From expert digital marketing strategies to the gritty challenges founders face, we share what works (and what d

recent_episodes.title: Welcome Flows, Retention & Revenue: The Email Deep Dive for Scaling Food & Drink Brands
recent_episodes.description:
Email has been declared “dead” more times than we can count. But inside strong eCommerce food & drink brands? It’s still driving 25-40% of total revenue, and often the highest margin revenue too.

In Episode 19 of Fuelling Foodies, we go deep into what’s actually converting in 2026 and why email remains the most controllable, profitable channel you own.

Here’s what we cover:

Why “email is dead” is one of the most damaging myths in eCommerceOwned audience vs rented audience (and

recent_episodes.title: The Future Of Non-Alcoholic Wine: Growth, Taste & The Vino Zero Story
recent_episodes.description:
Non-alcoholic wine is one of the fastest-growing categories in food & drink, but it’s also one of the most misunderstood.

In this episode of Fuelling Foodies, Kate sits down with Fiona & Alex, founders of Vino Zero, to unpack what’s really happening inside the non-alc wine space, from quality and consumer scepticism to explosive category growth and bold founder decisions.

From landing their first four pallets to supplying major hospitality venues and high-profile events, this is t

recent_episodes.title: Reinventing Snacking: How Popcorn Kitchen Launched Crunch Corn & Entered a New Category
recent_episodes.description:
Launching a new product is risky, especially in snacking.

In this episode of Fuelling Foodies: The Food Marketing Podcast, Kate sits down with Louise, Founder of Popcorn Kitchen, to unpack the real story behind the launch of Crunch Corn - from spotting white space in the savoury snacking market to building momentum in Q1 and beyond.

This is a founder-level conversation about innovation, timing, and what actually matters when you’re expanding into a new category, without losing wha

score_reason: Targets food brand founders and marketers, aligns directly with Andreas's Nourish Food Marketing experience and strategic insights.

key_themes: Scaling food & drink brands with limited budgets and resources
Owned audience channels (email, community) vs. paid/rented channels
Real founder stories: wins, failures, and honest lessons learned
Data-driven marketing strategies that actually convert (not vanity metrics)
Category innovation and repositioning in competitive food markets
enrichment.relevant_episode: Why Most Food Brands Get Marketing Wrong (And How to Fix It)! — Andreas's practitioner approach to cutting through marketing myths and exposing what actually drives growth aligns perfectly with this debut episode's mission to flip the script on food marketing conventional wisdom.
enrichment.coverage_gap: How AI and data analytics are reshaping food brand decision-making and competitive advantage—the podcast has focused heavily on marketing tactics and channel strategy but hasn't explored how founders can use AI and synthetic research to understand consumer behavior beyond what they say they do (the 'say-do gap').
enrichment.suggested_hook: From food agency founder to AI strategist: How traditional food brands can stop guessing and start using AI to actually understand what their customers will buy (not what they claim they will).
